name: threadlight-skills
description: Threadlight pilot pipeline — 16 pipeline skills + threadlight-auto orchestrator (17 total) that take a customer engagement from brief to deployed, production-ready Foundry agent. Covers process design (SPEC.md, demo deck, prep guide), local test (Pattern 0 quickstart), 7-phase azd deploy, pre/post-deploy gate, demo data factory, event triggers (ACA Jobs, Event Grid, cron), human-in-the-loop gates (Teams Adaptive Cards, audit trail), operator workspace UI, post-deploy Azure consumption projection + SKU-diff recommender (consumption-iq), an executable Responsible-AI-for-Foundry control plane (Discover -> Protect -> Govern) made of three new legs — threadlight-evals (offline batch via foundry-evals + Foundry Continuous Evaluation on live threads + A/B champion-challenger gate), threadlight-redteam (AI Red Teaming Agent / PyRIT adversarial scan for jailbreak / prompt-injection / exfiltration / harmful-content), and threadlight-govern (wraps foundry-agt: agent-runtime governance policy + in-process middleware + committed verifier report) that production-ready verifies actually ran rather than only scoring whether they were declared, the paved path to production (Citadel spoke + AGT v4 + AI Gateway / VNet checklist with live Azure probes), a CI/CD pipeline generator for locked-down customer envs (threadlight-cicd: GitHub Actions + Azure DevOps prod-deploy pipelines via OIDC / Workload Identity Federation, UAMI + federated-credential / least-privilege RBAC / private-VNet runner runbooks, onboarding-path gate, and a central-platform boundary that keeps the pilot pipeline separate from citadel-hub-deploy), a fork-and-customize final leg for onboarding the pipeline into one specific customer's environment (threadlight-customize: an instructions/runbooks skill — intake gate that captures customer documents, environment setup, requirements, and mandated template code into a customer-profile workbook; a customization map classifying each skill as customer-agnostic vs needs-per-customer-override with production onboarding flagged priority; a test-in-customer-environment runbook for fully-private VNet envs via Azure ML compute instance + VS Code or GitHub Codespaces; an explicit non-coverage boundary; plus a fork-runbook with upstream pin + overlay so customer changes survive upstream updates — deliberately NOT automation), an offline self-improvement leg that turns a finished CI run into a grounded learnings digest — phase parity, failure taxonomy, and recommendations — plus an optional paired model-router cost/quality scorecard from Azure Monitor token metrics (threadlight-router-bench, the Improve leg), and a full-auto orchestrator that drives the chain end-to-end behind one freeform prompt. Also bridges Kratos-exported agent projects: detect a Kratos export (src/hosted-agent/ + use-cases/<x>/), enrich/validate without regenerating the runtime, backfill evals/, and layer in the production-hardening skills (see docs/KRATOS-BRIDGE.md). USE FOR: threadlight, pilot pipeline, process design, demo deck, prep guide, local test, azd deploy, safe check, demo data, event triggers, HITL, workspace UI, consumption projection, cost projection, SKU diff, SKU recommendation, PAYG vs PTU, Azure retail pricing, load profile, evals, online eval, continuous evaluation, A/B evaluation, champion challenger, AI red teaming, red team scan, PyRIT, jailbreak, prompt injection, data exfiltration, agent-runtime governance, AGT middleware, responsible AI, RAI for Foundry, discover protect govern, production readiness, paved path to production, citadel spoke, AGT, AI gateway, lab graveyard, customer architecture review, CISO sign-off, go-live readiness, seller enablement, ci/cd pipeline, github actions pipeline, azure devops pipeline, OIDC, workload identity federation, federated credentials, UAMI, least-privilege RBAC, self-hosted runners, managed devops pool, private vnet runners, restricted environment deploy, platform team handoff, central platform boundary, one-prompt threadlight, autopilot, orchestrator, resume failed deploy, Kratos export, Kratos-exported project, foundry-agent.zip, compose on Kratos export, Kratos bridge, enrich exported agent, backfill evals, fork threadlight, clone threadlight, customize threadlight, customer fork, customer onboarding, production onboarding, onboard a customer, adapt the pipeline, tailor threadlight, customization map, what to fork vs keep, intake workbook, customer profile, mandated template code, test in customer environment, test on their tenant, private vnet dev loop, fully private environment, github codespaces, azure ml compute instance, azure ml vscode, private endpoint reachability, upstream pin, overlay merge, non-coverage boundary, learn from CI run, self-improving cold-path, CI failure taxonomy, model-router cost, token cost vs baseline, cost scorecard, learnings digest, run retro, final leg. DO NOT USE FOR: Foundry agent reference (use awesome-gbb), Zava digital-clone (use zava-constellation), central Citadel hub / shared AI gateway deployment (use citadel-hub-deploy in awesome-gbb).
